excerpt: A NERAI Political Risk Forecast by Serhan Ayik assessing whether the 2026 US midterm elections function as an institutional release valve or become a contested site of democratic stress. The brief evaluates the current US political-crisis signal cluster, including elevated authoritarianism, pressure-on-parties, repression-tactics and Newark curfew indicators, alongside the US-Iran variable and market-pricing divergence. Three forward scenarios are assessed through Q1 2027: Contained Polarisation 60% base case, Jammed Release Valve 25% worst case, and Accelerated Consolidation 15% tail risk. Probabilistic framing only — not a deterministic prediction.
title: The Release Valve Test: A NERAI Probability Forecast for the 2026 US Midterms and the Political-Crisis Signal Trajectory Through Q1 2027

excerpt: Mainstream commentary frames Pakistan's authoritarianism spike as imminent military takeover. The NERAI data tell a different story: this is institutional codification — the 27th Amendment (passed November 2025) embeds military influence through constitutional rewrite. Equilibrium-phase consolidation analogous to Turkey post-2016 or Hungary post-2010, not Egypt 2011 or Sudan 2019. Three scenarios (60%/25%/15%), 14-day watch list, full counter-thesis. Probabilistic framing only.
title: Codification, Not Coup: A NERAI Probability Forecast for Pakistan's Hybrid Authoritarian Equilibrium Through Mid-2027

Schotte's Credendo Risk Insight gave us the denominator — debt-service ratios, bilateral-creditor composition, the China–Paris Club coordination deficit. This brief layers the numerator: NERAI political-event signals that determine when and how debt vulnerability converts into distress. Kenya, Ghana, Ethiopia — three transmission channels, one synchronised −56%/−60%/−64% collapse in 12-month donor-support forecasts. Includes 14-day watch list with monitoring thresholds for trade-credit insurers and Paris Club coordinators.
title: Beyond the Debt Numbers: A Political & Event-Signal Layer on Sub-Saharan Africa's Debt-Distress Triggers (Q2 2026)

excerpt: A 24-page probability forecast by Serhan Ayik. NERAI's Russia Coup Index at 53.5/100 (95th percentile, 90-day rising) with the 12-month forecast declining -36% (0.110 → 0.070) — the diagnostic signature of managed securitisation, not regime decay. Securitisation-as-Stabilisation framework: heightened threat perception channeled into visible institutional response (purges, FSO expansion, military resource reallocation) reinforces elite coordination rather than triggering cascading defection. Three forward scenarios (Managed Securitisation 35%, Constrained Escalation 45%, Rapid Consolidation 20%) with full counter-thesis (Kendall-Taylor / Snegovaya cascade interpretation) and rebuttal. Probabilistic framing only — not a deterministic prediction.
title: The Securitization Equilibrium: A NERAI Probability Forecast for Russian Regime Stability and Coup Risk Through May 2027
